text.title: Collaborative Pattern Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns that recur in collaborative multi-agent runs, with detector + fix.

antipattern.description: Synthesizer is also a contributor — final output is biased toward that contributor's own ideas.
antipattern.reason: Detect: validator finds synthesizer.name ∈ contributors[*].name. Fix: hire a dedicated synthesizer agent on a different model family (e.g., opus or GPT-class while contributors are sonnet).

antipattern.description: All contributors on the same model family produce correlated outputs; iteration converges to one answer instead of preserving diversity.
antipattern.reason: Detect: pairwise cosine similarity of iteration-2 outputs > 0.85. Fix: mix model families (Claude + GPT + Gemini), or inject role-specific contrarian seeds into system prompts ("you privately disagree with the consensus; surface objections").

antipattern.description: No budget guard — N agents × M iterations silently drains tokens; the run completes but at 5× the planned cost.
antipattern.reason: Detect: post-run audit shows total_tokens > spec.budget.total_tokens. Fix: add token counter after each iteration; abort the loop and proceed to synthesis when used ≥ 0.9 × budget.

antipattern.description: Prompt inflation — iteration prompt accumulates full contribution history; iteration N pays for N copies of every prior output.
antipattern.reason: Detect: per-iteration input tokens grow roughly linearly with iteration index. Fix: per iteration, pass only the latest entry per agent, capped at ~3000 tokens total context per round.

antipattern.description: Race condition on workspace — async-running contributors write to the workspace concurrently; one entry overwrites another.
antipattern.reason: Detect: workspace entries with duplicate `(agent, iteration)` keys or non-monotonic `ts`. Fix: serialize writes via a single-writer queue or use an append-only log (e.g., SQLite WAL, NDJSON file) with a lock.
